element.description: It falls to Rhonas to instill this teaching in those who would enter the afterlife—but to his mind, the words themselves don't matter. Strength can't be taught. It must be built through practice and training. Rhonas demonstrates his teachings by his example, rather than by giving his students any kind of academic instruction. He welcomes the people of Naktamun to stand by the Hekma and watch him as he storms into the desert to battle the greatest horrors. He encourages them to observe his indomitable strength, for though they will never equal it, they can aspire to mimicry. He invites them to scrutinize every move and practice what they see
element.name: Strength Domain
element.sheet: The Strength domain is granted to those who follow the teachings of the God-Pharaoh Rhonas.

element.description:
You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Strength Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

Strength Domain Spells
Cleric LevelSpells
1stdivine favor, shield of faith
3rdenhance ability, protection from poison
5thhaste, protection from energy
7thdominate beast, stoneskin
9thdestructive wave, insect plague
element.name: Domain Spells

element.description: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency with heavy armor.
element.name: Bonus Proficiencies

sheet.content: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

element.description:
At 1st level, you learn one druid cantrip of your choice.

You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Athletics, Nature, or Survival.
element.name: Acolyte of Strength

element.description: At 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to enhance your physical might. When you make an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw using Strength, you can use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails.
element.name: Channel Divinity: Feat of Strength

sheet.content: When you make an attack roll/Ability check/saving throw with strength you can use add 10 to the roll before the result is revealed.

element.description: At 6th level, when a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw using Strength, you can use your reaction to grant that creature a +10 bonus to the roll, using your Channel Divinity. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fail.
element.name: Channel Divinity: Rhona's Blessing

sheet.content: When a creature within 30 ft of you makes an attack roll/ability check/saving throw with strength you can use your reaction to add 10 to the roll before the results are revealed.

element.description: At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 of the same damage type to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.
element.name: Divine Strike
element.sheet: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 of the same damage type to the target. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 2d8 of the same damage type to the target.

element.description: At 17th level, you gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.
element.name: Avatar of Battle
element.sheet: You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

info.description: Hazoret: Strength Domain for Clerics from Planeshift Amonkhet
info.name: Planeshift Amonkhet: Strength Domain
